originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and gears scripts, then initializes many inline rotational-motion, torque, tooth-profile, and transmission sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the opening, ratio, and compound-gear scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with animated spinning and angular-velocity scenes, then continues through disc-contact, torque, and gear-ratio sections driven by sliders and synchronized canvas updates.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening motion scenes, the play-pause controls, the angular-velocity slider interactions, and the later ratio and torque demonstrations under the original published runtime.
notes: The route preserves the published slider-driven explanations for angular velocity, torque, and tooth-size-adjusted ratio scenes.
The animations still use the original play-pause controls rather than replacement local UI.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one opening slider scene, one play-pause path, and one ratio scene as smoke-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening angular-velocity scene redraws, a play-pause control toggles, and the ratio scene updates from slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article transitions from pitch circles into tangent-and-normal geometry, involute profiles, and force-offset sections with sliders and synchronized geometry views.
localStatus: The local route keeps the tangent-normal, involute, and force-geometry sections intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The route preserves the published progression from pitch circles to involute tooth shapes rather than collapsing the middle sections into static diagrams.
Ciechanowski cross-links in the public body are rewritten locally when available or neutralized when no local replica exists.
evidence: Batch policy hardening introduced explicit audit coverage for ordinary in-body Ciechanowski article links.
The local route keeps the original geometry sections while the public-surface audit enforces footer-only provenance.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with multi-gear train examples, a four-gear reduction scene, a jammed-gears segmented-control example, and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later multi-gear and jam scenes through the original scripts while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one later compound-gear scene as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the four-gear scene remains interactive and the route stays responsive without upstream runtime dependencies.
